title: Lily and the Missing Watering Hole
synopsis: When all the animals in the savanna are thirsty because their watering hole has mysteriously disappeared, young explorer Lily teams up with her animal friends to solve the puzzle and discover what happened to their precious water source.

pages.text: Lily loved exploring the African savanna with her family. She had long curly auburn hair that bounced when she walked, and her green eyes sparkled with curiosity. Today felt different though. Something was wrong.

illustration.description: A young girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes walking through golden savanna grass under an orange sunset sky, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes and warm colors
illustration.alt: Lily exploring the savanna at sunset

pages.text: As Lily walked toward the big watering hole where all the animals drank, she noticed something strange. The elephants looked sad. The zebras seemed worried. Even the tall giraffes hung their heads low.

illustration.description: Sad savanna animals - elephants, zebras, and giraffes - painted in gouache with expressive faces and thick brushstrokes, with the girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes observing them in warm savanna colors
illustration.alt: Sad animals in the savanna with Lily watching

pages.text: When Lily reached the watering hole, she gasped. It was completely empty! Where once there had been cool, clear water, now there was only dry, cracked earth. 'Where did all the water go?' Lily wondered aloud.

illustration.description: An empty, cracked watering hole painted in gouache with textured brushstrokes, showing the girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes standing at the edge looking puzzled, in warm orange and golden tones
illustration.alt: Lily looking at the empty watering hole

pages.text: A wise old elephant named Tembo walked up to Lily. 'Little one,' he said with his deep, rumbly voice, 'we haven't had water for two days. All the animals are getting very thirsty. Can you help us solve this mystery?'

illustration.description: A large, wise elephant with expressive eyes talking to the girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes, painted in gouache with bold confident strokes and warm savanna colors under golden grass
illustration.alt: Tembo the elephant talking to Lily

pages.text: Lily loved solving puzzles. She put on her detective hat and started looking for clues. First, she walked all around the empty watering hole, looking very carefully at the ground.

illustration.description: The girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes wearing a detective hat, crouching down examining the ground around the watering hole, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes and textured painterly style
illustration.alt: Lily examining clues around the watering hole

pages.text: Suddenly, Lily spotted something interesting. There were strange marks in the mud - like someone had been digging! The marks led away from the watering hole toward a group of big rocks.

illustration.description: Muddy paw prints and digging marks leading toward large rocks, with the girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes following the trail, painted in warm savanna colors with bold gouache brushstrokes
illustration.alt: Lily following mysterious marks in the mud

pages.text: A chattering monkey named Kesi swung down from a tree. 'Lily!' she called. 'I saw something yesterday! There were some animals digging by those rocks over there. They had long claws and striped fur!'

illustration.description: An expressive monkey with bright eyes swinging from a tree branch talking to the girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes and warm orange sunset colors
illustration.alt: Kesi the monkey giving Lily information

pages.text: Lily's green eyes grew wide. 'Animals with long claws and striped fur? That sounds like meerkats! But why would meerkats dig near our watering hole?' She decided to investigate the rocks where Kesi had seen the digging.

illustration.description: The girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes looking thoughtful with her finger on her chin, standing near large rocks painted in gouache with textured painterly strokes and golden grass around her
illustration.alt: Lily thinking about the mystery clues

pages.text: As Lily approached the big rocks, she heard a funny sound - drip, drop, drip, drop. Following the sound, she discovered a small stream of water trickling between the rocks. But something was blocking most of it!

illustration.description: The girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes discovering a small stream between large rocks, painted in gouache with warm savanna colors and thick brushstrokes showing water trickling through stone
illustration.alt: Lily finding water trickling between rocks

pages.text: Suddenly, three little meerkat heads popped up from behind the rocks. 'Oh no!' squeaked the smallest one. 'We've been caught!' The meerkats looked very sorry and a little scared.

illustration.description: Three cute meerkats with expressive faces popping up from behind rocks, looking sorry, with the girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes nearby, painted in gouache with bold confident strokes and textured style
illustration.alt: Three guilty-looking meerkats appearing from behind rocks

pages.text: 'We didn't mean to cause trouble,' explained the mama meerkat. 'We were trying to dig a burrow for our babies, but we accidentally blocked the underground stream that feeds your watering hole. We're so sorry!'

illustration.description: A mama meerkat with baby meerkats explaining to the girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes, painted in warm gouache colors with expressive animal faces and thick brushstrokes under golden grass
illustration.alt: Mama meerkat explaining the accident to Lily

pages.text: Lily smiled kindly. 'It's okay! Accidents happen. Let's work together to fix this!' With help from Tembo the elephant, Kesi the monkey, and the meerkat family, they carefully moved the rocks and dirt that were blocking the stream.

illustration.description: The girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes working alongside the elephant, monkey, and meerkats to clear rocks and dirt, painted in gouache with warm savanna colors and textured painterly brushstrokes
illustration.alt: All the animals working together to fix the stream

pages.text: Soon, the water began flowing freely again! The watering hole filled up with cool, clear water. All the animals cheered and drank happily. Lily had solved her first savanna mystery, and she felt very proud.

illustration.description: The watering hole full of clear water again with happy animals drinking - elephants, zebras, giraffes, meerkats, and monkeys - while the girl with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes watches with satisfaction, painted in gouache with bold strokes and orange sunset colors
illustration.alt: All animals celebrating around the restored watering hole with Lily

metadata.illustrationStyle: gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes

questions.question: What was wrong with the watering hole when Lily first saw it?
questions.explanation: The watering hole was completely empty with only dry, cracked earth where the water used to be.

questions.question: Which animal first asked Lily for help?
questions.explanation: Tembo the wise old elephant was the first to ask Lily to help solve the mystery of the missing water.

questions.question: What clues did Lily find around the watering hole?
questions.explanation: Lily found strange digging marks in the mud that led away from the watering hole toward some big rocks.

questions.question: Why did the meerkats block the stream?
questions.explanation: The meerkats accidentally blocked the underground stream while trying to dig a burrow for their babies.

questions.question: How did all the animals solve the problem?
questions.explanation: Lily, Tembo, Kesi, and the meerkat family all worked together to move the rocks and dirt blocking the stream.
